sections.paragraph:
ἀλείφω fut. 2 sg. ἀλείψεις Ex 40:15, 1 aor. ἤλειψα, pf. inf. ἀληλιφέναι (Just., D. 86, 2). Mid.: fut. ἀλείψομαι 4 Km 4:2; 1 aor. impv. ἄλειψαι; pf. ptc. ἠλειμμένοι Num 3:3 (Hom.+)

① lit. to anoint by applying a liquid such as oil or perfume, anoint (Diod S 17, 90, 2) τοὺς πόδας μύρῳ anoint his feet w. perfume Lk 7:38, 46 (KWeiss, ZNW 46, ’55, 241–45; WClarke, ClJ 87, ’91/92, 257–63); J 12:3; cp. 11:2. Those who were ill were anointed w. oil (household remedy; cp. Cat. Cod. Astr. VII p. 178, 3; 28; TestAdam p. 122, 7; cp. 116, 10) Mk 6:13; Js 5:14 (MMeinertz, D. Krankensalbung Jk 5:14f: BZ 20, ’32, 23–36; CArmerding, BiblSacra 95, ’38, 195–201; HFriesenhahn, BZ 24, ’40, 185–90. S. ἔλαιον 1a and κάμνω 2 and 3). Of the dead, w. spices Mk 16:1. Mid. anoint oneself (Jos., Bell. 5, 565) τὴν κεφαλήν Mt 6:17 (w. washing of the face as Plut., Mor. 142a).

② to besmear with someth. undesirable, besmear fig. (Philo, Conf. Lingu. 91, Mos. 1, 298) pass. (on the permissive pass. s. Gildersleeve, Syntax I §167) ἀ. δυσωδίαν let oneself be besmeared w. filth of accepting false doctrine IEph 17:1 (ἀ. w. acc. of that which one applies, as 2 Km 14:2; Mi 6:15 al.). S. χρίω.—DELG. M-M. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

inf. inf. = infinitive

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

impv. impv. = imperative

ptc. ptc. = participle

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

ClJ ClJ = Classical Journal (Chicago)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

TestAdam TestAdam = Testament of Adam, II–III a.d.—List 5

BZ BZ = Biblische Zeitschrift—List 6

BiblSacra BiblSacra = Bibliotheca Sacra, A Theological Quarterly—List 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Gildersleeve, Syntax Gildersleeve, Syntax = BG./CMiller, Syntax of Classical Greek—List 6

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

acc. acc. = accusative

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 41
